Kruger National Park Private Safari Packages

a Slower more immersive safari experience and the best thing about it, its just you. Our Kruger National Park private safaris are designed around your interests and pace. Want to wait for that perfect shot, or try and see if the leopard will eventually skulk out of its comfy hidden spot? No problem, it's your safari. We offer full day and split day game drives, so you can choose the how long you would like to spend in the bush each day.

During your visit we will stay inside Kruger National Park rest camps, comfy rustic accommodations that allow you to be in the heart of South Africas great lowveld region. Staying inside the park allows us to venture out earlier and stay out later, giving you the opportunity to spot the mammals that move around in these times.

Why choose our Kruger National Park private safari packages?

Kruger National Park is one of those places, once you go there, there is a certain type of bug that bites, creating a longing once you leave. Our Kruger National Park private safari packages are designed for travelers who want to have a truly immersive safari experience. We only do longer safaris so that you will not feel rushed, we want you to have the time to slow down and take in all the sights, smells, and sounds of the great lowveld region.

Here's what sets our private Kruger National Park packages apart:

  • Fully Private Safaris - No shared vehicles. It’s just you, your guide, and the wild.
  • Flexible Itineraries – Choose full-day or split-day game drives to suit your pace.
  • Comfy Accommodation – Stay inside the park in authentic Kruger rest camps.
  • Seamless Travel – Pickup from Nelspruit or East Gate Airport with all park fees covered.

With our personalized service, local expertise, and exceptional value, these packages are ideal for travelers who prefer more freedom over their dining choices while still enjoying an immersive private Kruger National Park safari.

Elephant feasting on some grass in dry vegetation, in Kruger National Park Safari

What's included in our Kruger National Park private safari packages?

We've designed our Kruger National Park private safari packages to provide everythig else except meals and conservation fees, check out our private all-inclusive packages if you want meals included. This allows you to have more freedom to choose where and what you want to eat.

  • Private guided game drives (morning, afternoon, or full-day)
  • Accommodation inside Kruger National Park
  • Daily bottled water, coffee, and tea
  • Nearby airport or hotel pickup and drop-off
  • Flexible pacing and custom itinerary options
  • Birdwatching and photography opportunities on request

With our personalised service, local expertise, and exceptional value, these packages are ideal for travelers, honeymooners, and families who prefer more freedom over their dining choices and not having your guide in your face all the time.

Crested Barbet resting in mid day heat.

What Makes Our Kruger National Park Private Safaris Special?

Our Kruger National Park private safari packages gives you the freedom to do what you want to do. The vehicles are not shared, so you can take your time at sightings, and not feel rushed to move on because the colors of the Lilac-Breasted Roller are hypnotic. The Kruger National Park is a huge park, and there is much to see and explore, with our private safaris you can choose wether you want to do a full day game drive, or split day drives, which allows you to take a break mid-day, especially during the hotter months.

Looking over the Sabi River on the bridge near Skukuza Rest Camp

When to Visit Kruger National Park?

This is a good question, although Kruger National Park is excellent year round, the best time of course depends on what you want to see and experience. Kruger National Park has two main seasons, the dry season and then the green season. Both these seasons have their own appeal the drier months provide better game viewing and dramatic landscapes, while the green season offers lush scenery, abundant birdlife and heat.

Dry Season (May to September)

The dry season is considered the classic safari season, and for good reason. With the vegetation thinned out and the grass a bit sparse, animals are easier to spot as they gather around waterholes and rivers, making finding the big 5 and predators much easier. The cooler mornings and golden evening light also makes for amazing photos.

Green Season (October to April)

The rains clouds start showing themselves in October, with the first heavy rains usually falling in November. With the rains comes the birds, migrants from Europe and intra-African migrants following the rain. In January and February the park is alive with baby animals, but the rains can get a bit hectic, with some roads, rivers becoming impassable. But the park is still open and the animals are still here.

Male Impala at the end of the dry season in Kruger National Park Private Safari Package.

Book Your Kruger National Park Private Safari

If you have read this far, you don't need to go any further than the email link below. Unless you are interested in our private safari packages, which is a more flexible option. Booking our Kruger National Park Private safari package is easy, send an email to Eugene and he will start your booking process. We will ask you a few questions about you and your interests, and the dates you are interested in and check availability.

Do not hesitate to ask any questions, we are here to help you have the best possible safari experience in Kruger National Park.
